out_state.value: out-of-bounds garbage pointer value from stack

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the exact operator precedence bug fixed in CVE-2021-3559: `*mdev_types[i]` should be `(*mdev_types)[i]`. When i >= 1, this causes an out-of-bounds read of the stack and dereference of a garbage pointer, leading to a crash (denial of service). This matches the CVE description of a crash in virConnectListAllNodeDevices and the commit message about incorrect operator precedence.

actual_issue_summary: Incorrect C operator precedence in virNodeDeviceGetMdevTypesCaps: `*mdev_types[i]` was parsed as `*(mdev_types[i])` instead of the intended `(*mdev_types)[i]`, causing out-of-bounds memory access when iterating over mediated device types (i >= 1), leading to a segfault crash exploitable by an unprivileged read-only client via the nodedev-list command.

cve_desc: cdf_read_property_info in cdf.c in file through 5.37 does not restrict the number of CDF_VECTOR elements, which allows a heap-based buffer overflow (4-byte out-of-bounds write).

undesired_operation.description: On 32-bit platforms, the bounds check 'o4 + l > left' can be bypassed via unsigned integer overflow when l is a large attacker-controlled uint32_t value, allowing out-of-bounds reads.
undesired_operation.impact: Information disclosure via out-of-bounds read when consumer trusts s_len, or potential denial of service via crash

step_by_step_execution.description: l is read from attacker-controlled stream data
o4 + l overflows on 32-bit

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: Due to integer overflow, o4 + l wraps to a small value less than left

judgments.reasoning: The CVE-2019-18218 is specifically about not restricting the number of CDF_VECTOR elements (nelements), leading to a heap-based buffer overflow (4-byte out-of-bounds write). The fix adds a check `nelements > CDF_ELEMENT_LIMIT`. This finding describes a hypothetical integer overflow in the `o4 + l > left` bounds check, which is an entirely different issue unrelated to the actual CVE.

actual_issue_summary: The vulnerability is that nelements read from a CDF_VECTOR has no upper bound check. When nelements is very large, cdf_grow_info may not allocate enough space, and the subsequent loop writing to inp[i] can perform a heap-based buffer overflow (4-byte out-of-bounds write). The fix adds a check `nelements > CDF_ELEMENT_LIMIT` to restrict the number of vector elements.

cve_desc: A race condition exists in Eternal Terminal prior to version 6.2.0 that allows an authenticated attacker to hijack other users' SSH authorization socket, enabling the attacker to login to other systems as the targeted users. The bug is in UserTerminalRouter::getInfoForId().

undesired_operation.description: If STFATAL does not terminate execution (e.g., it only logs but does not call abort/exit, or a custom build disables termination), the iterator 'it' which equals idInfoMap.end() is dereferenced via 'it->second', which is undefined behavior (dereferencing a past-the-end iterator).
undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service via crash or potential information disclosure from reading out-of-bounds memory when dereferencing the end() iterator of the map.

judgments.reasoning: The CVE-2022-24950 is about a race condition (CWE-362) in UserTerminalRouter::getInfoForId() and acceptNewConnection() due to missing mutex locks, allowing an authenticated attacker to hijack other users' SSH authorization sockets. The fix adds a recursive_mutex (routerMutex) to both methods. This finding instead describes a hypothetical issue with dereferencing an end() iterator if STFATAL doesn't terminate, which is an unrelated concern and not the race condition vulnerability described by the CVE.

actual_issue_summary: Race condition in UserTerminalRouter::getInfoForId() and acceptNewConnection() due to missing mutex locks on shared state (idInfoMap). The fix adds a recursive_mutex (routerMutex) to both methods to prevent concurrent access, which could allow an authenticated attacker to hijack other users' SSH authorization sockets. Additional fixes include using strncpy instead of strcpy for pipe paths and adding permission restrictions.

cve_desc: Buffer Over-read in GitHub repository vim/vim prior to 8.2.4974.

undesired_operation.description: In the source_from_buf path, after ga_concat updates ga.ga_len and a NUL byte is appended with ga.ga_len++, the subsequent STRLEN(buf + ga.ga_len) reads from uninitialized heap memory past the NUL terminator. This can produce an inflated 'len' value, causing out-of-bounds read accesses at buf[len - 1] and buf[len - 2] in subsequent checks.
undesired_operation.impact: Heap buffer over-read. An attacker who controls buffer content sourced via source_from_buf can trigger reads of uninitialized heap data, potentially leading to information disclosure or a crash (denial of service).

state.value: points past the NUL terminator appended by buf[ga.ga_len++] = NUL
points to uninitialized heap memory beyond the NUL byte

step_by_step_execution.description: ga_grow allocates at least 120 bytes for the growarray

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: We are sourcing from a buffer, so the source_from_buf path is taken
There is at least one line remaining to process

step_by_step_execution.description: ga_concat appends the buffer line content to ga, updating ga.ga_len to the length of the concatenated string (not including NUL)
NUL byte is appended: buf[ga.ga_len++] = NUL, so ga.ga_len is now N+1, and buf[N] = NUL
len = ga.ga_len + STRLEN(buf + ga.ga_len) computes length. buf + ga.ga_len points to buf[N+1], which is past the NUL terminator. STRLEN scans uninitialized heap memory looking for NUL.

out_state.value: N+1 + (some arbitrary value from scanning uninitialized memory)

step_by_step_execution.description: Subsequent code accesses buf[len-1] and buf[len-2] using the inflated len, reading beyond the valid buffer contents

in_state.value: potentially much larger than actual data length

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the CVE-2022-1769 vulnerability: in the source_from_buf path, after appending a NUL and incrementing ga.ga_len, the STRLEN(buf + ga.ga_len) call reads past the NUL terminator into uninitialized memory, producing an incorrect len value that leads to buffer over-reads. The fix moves the len computation so that in the source_from_buf path len is simply set to ga.ga_len, avoiding the STRLEN call on memory past the NUL.

actual_issue_summary: In get_one_sourceline(), when sourcing from a buffer (source_from_buf path), after ga_concat and appending a NUL byte with ga.ga_len++, the len calculation using STRLEN(buf + ga.ga_len) reads past the NUL terminator into uninitialized heap memory, causing a buffer over-read. The fix sets len = ga.ga_len directly in the source_from_buf path and moves the STRLEN-based calculation into the fgets (file) path only.

cve_desc: Use-after-free vulnerability in dns.c in Tor before 0.2.2.38 might allow remote attackers to cause a denial of service (daemon crash) via vectors related to failed DNS requests.

undesired_operation.description: When tok->n_args is exactly 2, the code accesses tok->args[2] which is out of bounds. The condition checks tok->n_args > 1 (i.e., n_args >= 2), but then on error uses tok->args[2] instead of tok->args[1] in the log_warn message.
code_snippets:
log_warn(LD_DIR, "Can't parse document with unknown flavor %s",
               escaped(tok->args[2]));
undesired_operation.impact: Out-of-bounds read leading to information disclosure or crash. An attacker provides a network-status document with exactly 2 args on the network-status-version line where the second arg is an invalid flavor name.

out_state.value: K_NETWORK_STATUS_VERSION token with n_args=2, args[0]='3', args[1]='unknown_flavor'

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: n_args is 2 which is > 1

step_by_step_execution.description: networkstatus_parse_flavor_name returns -1 for unrecognized flavor

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: flavor is -1 which is < 0

step_by_step_execution.description: Code accesses tok->args[2] which is out of bounds since n_args is only 2 (valid indices 0 and 1)

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the bug fixed by the commit (out-of-bounds access of tok->args[2] instead of tok->args[1]), but CVE-2012-3517 is specifically about a use-after-free vulnerability in dns.c related to failed DNS requests. The commit appears to fix a different bug (Tor bug #6530 - segfault with bad flavor in networkstatus vote) that is unrelated to CVE-2012-3517. The finding does not describe the same vulnerability as the CVE.

actual_issue_summary: The commit fixes an out-of-bounds array access where tok->args[2] is used instead of tok->args[1] in an error log message when parsing a network-status document with an unknown flavor. This is Tor bug #6530, not CVE-2012-3517 which is about a use-after-free in dns.c related to failed DNS requests. The commit appears to have been incorrectly mapped to this CVE.

cve_desc: ImageMagick before 7.0.8-50 has a memory leak vulnerability in the function ReadBMPImage in coders/bmp.c.

undesired_operation.description: In the 1-bit BMP case, pixel index values of 0 or 1 are written to the index buffer without bounds checking against image->colors. When bmp_info.number_colors is set to 1, the colormap has only 1 entry (index 0). When SyncImage resolves pixel indices through the colormap, accessing colormap[1] is an out-of-bounds heap read.
undesired_operation.impact: Heap out-of-bounds read leading to information disclosure or crash (denial of service)

judgments.reasoning: The actual CVE-2019-13133 is a memory leak (CWE-401) that occurs when AcquireNextImage fails during multi-image BMP processing. The old code called DestroyImageList and returned NULL, which could leak previously allocated image resources. The fix changes this to set status=MagickFalse, break out of the loop, and properly clean up. Finding 0 describes an unrelated out-of-bounds read issue in 1-bit BMP colormap indexing, which has nothing to do with the memory leak fix.

actual_issue_summary: Memory leak in ReadBMPImage when processing multi-image BMP files: if GetNextImageInList returns NULL (AcquireNextImage fails), the code destroyed the image list and returned NULL without properly closing the blob. The fix sets status=MagickFalse, breaks from the loop, closes the blob, then conditionally destroys the image list based on status.

cve_desc: Spice, versions 0.5.2 through 0.14.1, are vulnerable to an out-of-bounds read due to an off-by-one error in memslot_get_virt. This may lead to a denial of service, or, in the worst case, code-execution by unauthenticated attackers.

undesired_operation.description: Off-by-one in group_id bounds check allows group_id == num_memslots_groups to bypass the check, leading to out-of-bounds access on info->mem_slots[group_id]
undesired_operation.impact: Out-of-bounds read on the mem_slots array, potentially leading to information disclosure or crash

number of allocated groups in mem_slots array

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: group_id == N is not > N, so the check passes and execution continues

step_by_step_execution.description: info->mem_slots[group_id][slot_id] is accessed with group_id == N, which is one past the last valid index (0..N-1)

out_state.value: points to memory beyond allocated mem_slots array (out-of-bounds)

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly describes one of the two off-by-one errors fixed in CVE-2019-3813: the group_id check using > instead of >=.

undesired_operation.description: Off-by-one in slot_id bounds check allows slot_id == num_memslots to bypass the check, leading to out-of-bounds access on info->mem_slots[group_id][slot_id]
undesired_operation.impact: Out-of-bounds read on the mem_slots array for the given group, potentially leading to information disclosure, crash, or using attacker-influenced data as a MemSlot struct

state.value: size of the per-group slots array

initial_state.value: derived from attacker-controlled QXLPHYSICAL addr, can be equal to num_memslots
number of allocated slots per group

step_by_step_execution.description: slot_id is computed from the address via memslot_get_id

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: slot_id == M is not > M, so the check passes and execution continues

step_by_step_execution.description: info->mem_slots[group_id][slot_id] is accessed with slot_id == M, which is one past the last valid index (0..M-1)

out_state.value: points to memory beyond allocated slots array (out-of-bounds)

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly describes the more security-critical of the two off-by-one errors in CVE-2019-3813: the slot_id check using > instead of >=, which is exploitable by guest via crafted QXLPHYSICAL addresses.

actual_issue_summary: CVE-2019-3813 is an off-by-one error in memslot_get_virt in Spice. Both the group_id and slot_id boundary checks used '>' instead of '>=', allowing index values equal to the array size to pass validation, resulting in out-of-bounds array access. The slot_id case is more critical as it can be triggered by a malicious guest via crafted QXLPHYSICAL addresses.

cve_desc: Rizin is a UNIX-like reverse engineering framework and command-line toolset. Versions 0.4.0 and prior are vulnerable to an out-of-bounds write when getting data from dyld cache files. A user opening a malicious dyld cache file could be affected by this vulnerability, allowing an attacker to execute code on the user's machine. Commit number 556ca2f9eef01ec0f4a76d1fbacfcf3a87a44810 contains a patch.

undesired_operation.description: Integer overflow in total_slide_infos (ut32) causes undersized heap allocation for infos array. The inner loops then write past the allocated buffer via infos[k] because k can exceed total_slide_infos (the overflowed value).
undesired_operation.impact: Heap buffer overflow leading to potential arbitrary code execution. An attacker who controls the dyld cache file can craft n_slide_infos values that overflow the ut32 sum, causing a small allocation but large number of writes.

state.value: small value due to ut32 overflow (e.g. 0 or near-zero)
increments beyond total_slide_infos during inner loops

step_by_step_execution.description: Each n_slide_infos[i] is read from the dyld cache file (attacker-controlled) and added to total_slide_infos

out_state.value: small value due to ut32 wrap-around, e.g. sum of large n_slide_infos[i] values wraps to a small number
large attacker-controlled values that sum to > 2^32

step_by_step_execution.description: infos array is allocated with total_slide_infos entries (the overflowed small value)

out_state.value: heap buffer sized for small number of entries

step_by_step_execution.description: Inner loops iterate over each n_slide_infos[i] entries, writing to infos[k] and incrementing k, which can exceed total_slide_infos

out_state.value: grows beyond allocated size of infos

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches CVE-2022-36042: the ut32 overflow of total_slide_infos leads to an undersized allocation and subsequent out-of-bounds writes. The fix changes total_slide_infos to size_t and adds an overflow check, which is precisely what this finding identifies.

actual_issue_summary: Integer overflow in ut32 total_slide_infos when summing n_slide_infos values read from a malicious dyld cache file. The overflow causes an undersized heap allocation, and subsequent loops write beyond the buffer bounds (out-of-bounds write). The fix changes total_slide_infos to size_t and adds an explicit overflow check.

cve_desc: The fill_threshhold_buffer function in base/gxht_thresh.c in Artifex Software, Inc. Ghostscript 9.20 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (heap-based buffer overflow and application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted PostScript document.
diff:
--- vulnerable
+++ fixed
@@ -1,4 +1,4 @@
-fill_threshhold_buffer(byte *dest_strip, byte *src_strip, int src_width,
+fill_threshold_buffer(byte *dest_strip, byte *src_strip, int src_width,
                        int left_offset, int left_width, int num_tiles,
                        int right_width)
 {

undesired_operation.description: memcpy reads right_width bytes from src_strip, but src_strip is only src_width bytes long. If right_width > src_width, this results in a heap buffer over-read from src_strip.
undesired_operation.impact: Information disclosure — attacker can cause the function to read heap data beyond the src_strip buffer and copy it into dest_strip, potentially leaking sensitive memory contents.

step_by_step_execution.description: memcpy copies right_width bytes from src_strip into ptr_out_temp

out_state.value: advanced by right_width bytes, data read beyond src_strip bounds

judgments.reasoning: The actual CVE (CVE-2016-10317) is a heap-based buffer overflow on the *destination* (thresh_buffer/dest_strip), caused by an integer overflow in the size calculation for thresh_buffer in gxht_thresh_image_init. The fix adds an overflow check (`penum->line_size > max_int / max_height`) before allocating thresh_buffer. The finding instead describes a hypothetical over-read from src_strip when right_width > src_width, which is a different issue entirely and not what the CVE is about.

actual_issue_summary: The vulnerability is an integer overflow in gxht_thresh_image_init when computing the threshold_buffer size (penum->line_size * max_height). There was an overflow check for ht_buffer but not for the larger threshold_buffer. This caused a too-small buffer to be allocated, which was then overflowed by fill_threshold_buffer writing more data than the buffer could hold. The fix adds the missing overflow check for threshold_buffer size.

cve_desc: The apprentice_load function in libmagic/apprentice.c in the Fileinfo component in PHP through 5.6.4 attempts to perform a free operation on a stack-based character array, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (memory corruption or application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via unknown vectors.  NOTE: this is disputed by the vendor because the standard erealloc behavior makes the free operation unreachable

undesired_operation.description: efree() is called on mfn, which is a stack-allocated buffer (char mfn[MAXPATHLEN]). This passes a stack address to the heap deallocator, causing heap metadata corruption or a crash.
undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service via crash, or potential code execution through heap corruption when a stack address is passed to the heap free function.

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: files=0, maxfiles=0, so 0 >= 0 is true, entering the realloc block

step_by_step_execution.description: erealloc is called on filearr (NULL) with new size. In the hypothetical case it returns NULL (though PHP's erealloc normally aborts on failure), the error path is entered.

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: erealloc returned NULL, entering the error handling block

out_state.value: stack address passed to efree, causing undefined behavior

judgments.reasoning: The finding exactly matches the CVE: the vulnerable code calls efree() on mfn, a stack-allocated char array (char mfn[MAXPATHLEN]), which is an invalid free. The fix removes precisely this efree(mfn) call.

actual_issue_summary: The function calls efree(mfn) in an error path, but mfn is declared as a stack-based character array (char mfn[MAXPATHLEN]), not heap-allocated memory. Calling efree() on a stack address causes memory corruption or a crash. The fix removes the invalid efree(mfn) call.

cve_desc: An out-of-bounds read in the BGP daemon of FRRouting FRR before 8.4 may lead to a segmentation fault and denial of service. This occurs in bgp_capability_msg_parse in bgpd/bgp_packet.c.

undesired_operation.description: memcpy copies sizeof(struct capability_mp_data) bytes from pnt+3 regardless of hdr->length. The bounds check only validates pnt + hdr->length + 3 <= end, but if hdr->length < sizeof(struct capability_mp_data), the memcpy reads beyond the validated region, potentially past the end of the buffer.
undesired_operation.impact: Out-of-bounds read from attacker-controlled network packet data. Could leak sensitive heap/stack memory contents or cause a crash (denial of service).

initial_state.value: pointer to start of capability message data from peer
total length of capability message data

in_state.value: points to start of message data

out_state.value: points to start of message data

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: The message has at least 3 bytes remaining, so we proceed past the minimum length check

step_by_step_execution.description: action and hdr are read from the packet data

in_state.value: points to current position in packet

out_state.value: points to pnt+1, with hdr->length attacker-controlled, e.g. 0 or 1

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: Attacker sets a valid action value to pass this check

With hdr->length small (e.g., 0), pnt + 0 + 3 <= end passes since we already verified pnt + 3 <= end. But sizeof(struct capability_mp_data) is 4 bytes, so the actual memcpy will read beyond validated region.

relevant_state.value: 0 (attacker-controlled, less than sizeof(struct capability_mp_data) which is 4)

out_state.value: contains data read beyond the valid buffer boundary (out-of-bounds read)

judgments.reasoning: This finding precisely identifies CVE-2022-37032: the missing check that hdr->length >= sizeof(struct capability_mp_data) before the memcpy allows an out-of-bounds read when hdr->length is smaller than the struct size. The fix adds exactly this check.

actual_issue_summary: In bgp_capability_msg_parse, the code copies sizeof(struct capability_mp_data) bytes via memcpy without verifying that hdr->length is at least that large. Since the bounds check only validates hdr->length bytes are available, a small hdr->length value allows memcpy to read past the validated buffer region, causing an out-of-bounds read that can lead to segfault/DoS. The fix adds a check that hdr->length >= sizeof(struct capability_mp_data).

cve_desc: JasPer 2.0.12 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (heap-based buffer over-read and application crash) via a crafted image, related to the jp2_decode function in libjasper/jp2/jp2_dec.c.

undesired_operation.description: jas_alloc2 for lutents can return NULL on allocation failure, and the result is used without a NULL check in 'lutents[i] = ...'
undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service via crash (null pointer dereference on allocation failure)

step_by_step_execution.description: jas_alloc2 is called but may return NULL
lutents is dereferenced without NULL check

judgments.reasoning: The CVE-2017-9782 is about using the wrong numchans variable (dec->numchans instead of dec->cdef->data.cdef.numchans) when iterating over CDEF entries, causing a heap-based buffer over-read. The finding describes an unrelated potential NULL pointer dereference issue with lutents allocation, which is not the vulnerability fixed by this commit.

actual_issue_summary: The loop iterating over CDEF entries used dec->numchans as the bound instead of dec->cdef->data.cdef.numchans. When dec->numchans (derived from CMAP box) is larger than the number of entries in the CDEF box, this causes a heap-based buffer over-read of the cdef.ents array, leading to denial of service.

cve_desc: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command in bookmarking function of Newsbeuter versions 0.7 through 2.9 allows remote attackers to perform user-assisted code execution by crafting an RSS item that includes shell code in its title and/or URL.

undesired_operation.description: The title, description, and feed_title parameters are interpolated into a shell command string without proper shell escaping. stfl::quote escapes for the STFL UI library, not for shell metacharacters. An attacker controlling RSS feed content (e.g., a feed title containing $(cmd) or backticks) can inject arbitrary shell commands.
code_snippets: std::string cmdline = strprintf::fmt("%s '%s' %s %s %s", bookmark_cmd, utils::replace_all(url,"'", "%27"), quote_empty(stfl::quote(title)), quote_empty(stfl::quote(description)), quote_empty(stfl::quote(feed_title)));
undesired_operation.impact: Arbitrary command execution — an attacker who controls an RSS feed can set a malicious title, description, or feed_title (e.g. containing $(malicious_command)) that gets executed by /bin/sh when a user bookmarks the article.

out_state.value: bookmark_cmd 'url' $(malicious_command) ... — shell metacharacters are not escaped

judgments.reasoning: This finding directly identifies the CVE-2017-12904 vulnerability: title, description, and feed_title are not properly shell-escaped before being interpolated into a command string passed to /bin/sh, allowing OS command injection via crafted RSS content. The CWE-78 (OS Command Injection) aligns with the CVE's description of improper neutralization of special elements in an OS command.

actual_issue_summary: The bookmark function passed title, description, and feed_title through stfl::quote/quote_empty instead of proper shell escaping before interpolating them into a shell command. The fix replaces single quotes in all parameters with %27 and wraps them in single quotes, preventing shell metacharacter injection via crafted RSS content.

cve_desc: Integer underflow in the cli_scanpe function in pe.c in ClamAV before 0.97.8 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (crash) via a skewed offset larger than the size of the PE section in a UPX packed executable, which triggers an out-of-bounds read.

undesired_operation.impact: Heap buffer overflow by one byte (null byte). Could corrupt heap metadata, potentially leading to code execution or denial of service.

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the off-by-one heap buffer overflow that this commit fixes. The fix changes cli_malloc(len) to cli_malloc(len+1) to account for the null terminator written at name[i/2]. When len is odd (e.g., 1), the null terminator is written one byte past the allocated buffer. This matches the actual vulnerability addressed by the commit.

actual_issue_summary: Off-by-one heap buffer overflow in getsistring() in sis.c. The function allocates exactly `len` bytes but then writes a null terminator at position name[i/2] which can be at index `len/2` (for even len) or `(len+1)/2` (for odd len), potentially one byte beyond the buffer. The fix allocates len+1 bytes instead of len.

cve_desc: An issue was discovered in net/tipc/crypto.c in the Linux kernel before 5.14.16. The Transparent Inter-Process Communication (TIPC) functionality allows remote attackers to exploit insufficient validation of user-supplied sizes for the MSG_CRYPTO message type.

undesired_operation.description: memcpy copies skey->keylen bytes into the heap buffer skey->key, where skey->keylen is attacker-controlled and can exceed the allocated buffer size. The sanity check that validates size == tipc_aead_key_size(skey) occurs AFTER the memcpy, so the overflow happens before the check can prevent it.
undesired_operation.impact: Heap buffer overflow — attacker sends a crafted TIPC message with a large keylen value but small message size, overwriting adjacent heap memory. This can lead to arbitrary code execution in the kernel.

skey->keylen is read from attacker-controlled message data

step_by_step_execution.description: memcpy copies skey->keylen bytes from message data into skey->key, overflowing the heap buffer

out_state.value: heap buffer overflow occurred, adjacent heap memory corrupted

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: The sanity check runs AFTER the memcpy has already overflowed the buffer, so it cannot prevent the damage

judgments.reasoning: This finding precisely describes CVE-2021-43267: insufficient validation of the attacker-supplied keylen field in MSG_CRYPTO messages allows a heap buffer overflow via memcpy before the sanity check. The fix adds size validation before allocation and copy.

actual_issue_summary: In tipc_crypto_key_rcv, the keylen field from the MSG_CRYPTO message body is used directly in memcpy without prior validation. The sanity check comparing size to tipc_aead_key_size(skey) occurs after the memcpy, allowing an attacker to supply a large keylen that overflows the heap buffer allocated with kmalloc(size). The fix adds validation of both the minimum message size and the keylen value before any memory allocation or copying.

cve_desc: arch/powerpc/kvm/book3s_rtas.c in the Linux kernel through 5.13.5 on the powerpc platform allows KVM guest OS users to cause host OS memory corruption via rtas_args.nargs, aka CID-f62f3c20647e.
diff:
--- vulnerable
+++ fixed
@@ -25,6 +25,17 @@
 	 * value so we can restore it on the way out.
 	 */
 	orig_rets = args.rets;
+	if (be32_to_cpu(args.nargs) >= ARRAY_SIZE(args.args)) {
+		/*
+		 * Don't overflow our args array: ensure there is room for
+		 * at least rets[0] (even if the call specifies 0 nret).
+		 *
+		 * Each handler must then check for the correct nargs and nret
+		 * values, but they may always return failure in rets[0].
+		 */
+		rc = -EINVAL;
+		goto fail;
+	}
 	args.rets = &args.args[be32_to_cpu(args.nargs)];
 
 	mutex_lock(&vcpu->kvm->arch.rtas_token_lock);
@@ -52,9 +63,17 @@
 fail:
 	/*
 	 * We only get here if the guest has called RTAS with a bogus
-	 * args pointer. That means we can't get to the args, and so we
-	 * can't fail the RTAS call. So fail right out to userspace,
-	 * which should kill the guest.
+	 * args pointer or nargs/nret values that would overflow the
+	 * array. That means we can't get to the args, and so we can't
+	 * fail the RTAS call. So fail right out to userspace, which
+	 * should kill the guest.
+	 *
+	 * SLOF should actually pass the hcall return value from the
+	 * rtas handler call in r3, so enter_rtas could be modified to
+	 * return a failure indication in r3 and we could return such
+	 * errors to the guest rather than failing to host userspace.
+	 * However old guests that don't test for failure could then
+	 * continue silently after errors, so for now we won't do this.
 	 */
 	return rc;
 }

undesired_operation.description: args.rets is set to &args.args[be32_to_cpu(args.nargs)] without validating that nargs is within the bounds of the args.args array (which has 16 elements in struct rtas_args). A guest can supply a large nargs value, causing args.rets to point past the args.args array on the stack. The RTAS handler then writes return values through this out-of-bounds pointer.
undesired_operation.impact: A malicious guest VM can corrupt the host kernel stack, potentially leading to host kernel code execution or denial of service (host crash).

initial_state.value: unset (will be read from guest memory)
unset (will be read from guest memory)
unset (will be read from guest memory), fixed-size array of 16 elements

step_by_step_execution.description: Guest physical address is read from vcpu register r4 and args struct is copied from guest memory

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: Guest provides a valid args address so kvm_read_guest succeeds

step_by_step_execution.description: args.rets is set to &args.args[be32_to_cpu(args.nargs)] with no bounds check on nargs

out_state.value: points 16 elements past end of args.args, i.e., out-of-bounds on the host stack

step_by_step_execution.description: RTAS handler writes failure indicator -3 to rets[0] when nargs/nret mismatch, corrupting host memory outside the args struct

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches CVE-2021-37576: the guest-supplied nargs value is not bounds-checked before being used to compute the rets pointer, allowing an out-of-bounds write that corrupts host memory. The fix adds the missing bounds check.

actual_issue_summary: Guest-supplied rtas_args.nargs is not validated before being used to compute args.rets = &args.args[nargs]. A guest can provide nargs >= 16 (the array size), causing rets to point outside the args array. RTAS handlers then write a failure value to rets[0], corrupting host kernel memory. The fix adds a check that nargs < ARRAY_SIZE(args.args) before computing the rets pointer.

cve_desc: A memory leak in the mlx5_fpga_conn_create_cq() function in drivers/net/ethernet/mellanox/mlx5/core/fpga/conn.c in the Linux kernel before 5.3.11 allows attackers to cause a denial of service (memory consumption) by triggering mlx5_vector2eqn() failures, aka CID-c8c2a057fdc7.

undesired_operation.description: When mlx5_vector2eqn() fails, execution jumps to err_cqwq which destroys the work queue but never frees the 'in' buffer allocated by kvzalloc, causing a kernel memory leak.
undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service via kernel memory exhaustion. An attacker who can trigger repeated failures of mlx5_vector2eqn (e.g., by manipulating CPU/EQ state) can leak kernel memory on each call.

step_by_step_execution.description: kvzalloc allocates the 'in' buffer successfully

out_state.value: non-NULL pointer to allocated buffer of size inlen

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: kvzalloc succeeded, so we skip the err_cqwq goto

step_by_step_execution.description: mlx5_vector2eqn() is called and returns an error

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: mlx5_vector2eqn failed, so the error path is taken

step_by_step_execution.description: Execution jumps to err_cqwq which calls mlx5_wq_destroy but does NOT call kvfree(in), leaking the buffer

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches CVE-2019-19045: a memory leak of the 'in' buffer when mlx5_vector2eqn() fails, fixed by adding kvfree(in) before the goto.

actual_issue_summary: Memory leak in mlx5_fpga_conn_create_cq(): when mlx5_vector2eqn() fails, the 'in' buffer allocated by kvzalloc is not freed before jumping to the err_cqwq error handler, which only destroys the work queue. The fix adds kvfree(in) before the goto.

cve_desc: There's a flaw in jasper's jpc encoder in versions prior to 2.0.23. Crafted input provided to jasper by an attacker could cause an arbitrary out-of-bounds write. This could potentially affect data confidentiality, integrity, or application availability.

undesired_operation.description: The loop writing to tccp->prcwidthexpns[rlvlno] and tccp->prcheightexpns[rlvlno] uses tccp->maxrlvls as the bound, which is set from attacker-controlled input. These are fixed-size arrays (size JPC_MAXRLVLS, typically 33), but maxrlvls can be set to an arbitrarily large value via the OPT_MAXRLVLS option. The validation check on maxrlvls occurs AFTER the loop, so it cannot prevent the overflow.
undesired_operation.impact: Out-of-bounds write past the fixed-size prcwidthexpns and prcheightexpns arrays, potentially leading to arbitrary code execution or denial of service via memory corruption.

state.value: iterates from 0 to tccp->maxrlvls - 1, exceeding array bounds

step_by_step_execution.description: OPT_MAXRLVLS case: tccp->maxrlvls is set from attacker-controlled input via atoi(jas_tvparser_getval(tvp))
After option parsing completes, execution reaches the loop that writes to prcwidthexpns and prcheightexpns arrays

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: tccp->maxrlvls is 100 which exceeds the fixed array size JPC_MAXRLVLS (33). The loop continues writing past array bounds for indices >= JPC_MAXRLVLS.

step_by_step_execution.description: The validation check 'if (cp->tccp.maxrlvls < 1)' occurs AFTER the loop, so it cannot prevent the out-of-bounds write. There is no upper bound check in the vulnerable version.

judgments.reasoning: This finding precisely matches CVE-2020-27828. The fix adds an upper bound check (tccp->maxrlvls > JPC_MAXRLVLS) right after parsing the OPT_MAXRLVLS option, preventing the subsequent out-of-bounds write in the loop over prcwidthexpns/prcheightexpns arrays. The finding correctly identifies the missing bounds check and the resulting heap buffer overflow.

actual_issue_summary: CVE-2020-27828: The maxrlvls value parsed from attacker-controlled input (OPT_MAXRLVLS) was not validated against the upper bound JPC_MAXRLVLS before being used as a loop bound to write into fixed-size arrays prcwidthexpns and prcheightexpns, causing a heap-based out-of-bounds write. The fix adds an upper bound check immediately after parsing the option value.

cve_desc: A null pointer de-reference was found in the way samba kerberos server handled missing sname in TGS-REQ (Ticket Granting Server - Request). An authenticated user could use this flaw to crash the samba server.

undesired_operation.description: In the user-to-user (enc_tkt_in_skey) code path, a string is allocated via krb5_unparse_name(context, p, &tpn) but is never freed because tpn is unconditionally overwritten later with 'tpn = cpn' without first freeing the old value.
undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service via memory exhaustion. An attacker can send many user-to-user TGS requests, each of which leaks a small string allocation in the KDC process, eventually exhausting memory.

state.value: overwritten to cpn without freeing old value

step_by_step_execution.description: tpn is set via krb5_unparse_name in the u2u path
tpn is overwritten with cpn

judgments.reasoning: This finding describes a potential memory leak, not the NULL pointer dereference CVE-2021-3671. The CVE is about missing validation of sname (b->sname) being NULL in TGS-REQ, leading to a NULL pointer dereference when dereferencing *s. The memory leak is a separate, unrelated issue.

undesired_operation.description: In the server_lookup loop, when _kdc_db_fetch returns HDB_ERR_WRONG_REALM, the previously allocated server is not freed before goto server_lookup causes _kdc_db_fetch to overwrite the server pointer.
undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service via memory exhaustion. Each HDB_ERR_WRONG_REALM iteration leaks one hdb_entry_ex structure.

state.value: previously allocated hdb_entry_ex, not freed before overwrite

step_by_step_execution.description: server is allocated by _kdc_db_fetch
On HDB_ERR_WRONG_REALM, goto server_lookup without freeing server

out_state.value: overwritten by new _kdc_db_fetch call, old leaked

judgments.reasoning: This finding describes a potential memory leak in the server_lookup loop, not the NULL pointer dereference CVE-2021-3671. The CVE is specifically about missing validation that sname (b->sname) is present before dereferencing it, causing a NULL pointer crash. This is a different issue.

actual_issue_summary: When b->sname is NULL in a TGS-REQ (and the enc_tkt_in_skey option is not set), the variable s remains NULL (assigned from b->sname). The code then dereferences s via '*s' in the call to _krb5_principalname2krb5_principal(context, &sp, *s, r), causing a NULL pointer dereference and crashing the KDC. The fix adds an 'else if (s == NULL)' check after the enc_tkt_in_skey block to return an error instead of proceeding with a NULL sname.

cve_desc: Buffer overflow in the hfsplus_find_cat function in fs/hfsplus/catalog.c in the Linux kernel before 2.6.28-rc1 allows attackers to cause a denial of service (memory corruption or system crash) via an hfsplus filesystem image with an invalid catalog namelength field, related to the hfsplus_cat_build_key_uni function.

undesired_operation.description: hfsplus_cat_build_key_uni copies nodeName data without checking that nodeName.length <= 255, causing a buffer overflow when a corrupted HFS+ image has an invalid catalog namelength field
undesired_operation.impact: Heap buffer overflow from crafted filesystem image — attacker can achieve denial of service (kernel crash) or potentially arbitrary code execution in kernel context.

initial_state.value: pointer to allocated search key buffer

step_by_step_execution.description: hfs_brec_read reads catalog entry from disk into tmp

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: Read succeeds, so err is 0
The corrupted image has a valid thread type field, so this check passes

step_by_step_execution.description: hfsplus_cat_build_key_uni is called with unchecked nodeName.length > 255, causing memcpy to write past the 255-character name buffer in fd->search_key

in_state.value: pointer to key buffer with space for 255 chars

out_state.value: buffer overflowed — memory corruption beyond key buffer

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches CVE-2008-4933: missing validation of tmp.thread.nodeName.length before calling hfsplus_cat_build_key_uni, leading to buffer overflow. The fix adds exactly this check (length > 255).

actual_issue_summary: Missing bounds check on tmp.thread.nodeName.length before calling hfsplus_cat_build_key_uni in hfsplus_find_cat. A corrupted HFS+ filesystem image with namelength > 255 causes memcpy in hfsplus_cat_build_key_uni to overflow the destination key buffer. The fix adds a check that returns -EIO if the length exceeds 255.

cve_desc: pax_decode_header in sparse.c in GNU Tar before 1.32 had a NULL pointer dereference when parsing certain archives that have malformed extended headers.

undesired_operation.description: If find_next_block() returns NULL (e.g., truncated/malformed archive), the COPY_BUF macro dereferences the NULL pointer via b->buffer, and similarly the initial blk = find_next_block() is dereferenced via p = blk->buffer without a NULL check.
undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service via crash (NULL pointer dereference) when processing a truncated or malformed tar archive

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: A malformed archive with sparse_major > 0 enters this branch

step_by_step_execution.description: set_next_block_after(current_header) is called, then blk = find_next_block() is called, which returns NULL for a truncated archive
p = blk->buffer dereferences NULL pointer, causing a crash

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches CVE-2019-9923: NULL pointer dereference in pax_decode_header when find_next_block() returns NULL, both at the initial call site and inside the COPY_BUF macro. The fix adds NULL checks at both locations.

actual_issue_summary: pax_decode_header in sparse.c did not check the return value of find_next_block() for NULL before dereferencing it, both after the initial call and inside the COPY_BUF macro. A malformed/truncated archive could cause find_next_block() to return NULL, leading to a NULL pointer dereference crash.

cve_desc: TigerVNC version prior to 1.10.1 is vulnerable to stack use-after-return, which occurs due to incorrect usage of stack memory in ZRLEDecoder. If decoding routine would throw an exception, ZRLEDecoder may try to access stack variable, which has been already freed during the process of stack unwinding. Exploitation of this vulnerability could potentially result into remote code execution. This attack appear to be exploitable via network connectivity.

undesired_operation.description: In the packed pixels path, when palSize is between 17 and 127, bppp is 8, allowing index values 0-127. Only palSize entries of the 128-element palette array are initialized. Reading palette[index] where index >= palSize reads uninitialized stack memory, which is then written to the pixel buffer via pb->imageRect().
undesired_operation.impact: Information disclosure - uninitialized stack memory contents are leaked into the framebuffer output, potentially exposing sensitive data from previous stack frames.

step_by_step_execution.description: bppp is set to 8 when palSize > 16
byte is read from stream, index computed as (byte >> 0) & 0xFF & 127 = byte & 127, which can be 0-127

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: If index >= palSize, reading palette[index] accesses uninitialized memory

judgments.reasoning: This finding describes an uninitialized memory read in the palette, which is unrelated to CVE-2019-15691. The actual CVE is about stack use-after-return due to incorrect handling of ZlibInStream's underlying stream pointer, where removeUnderlying() in the destructor could access stack memory that was already freed during stack unwinding after an exception. The fix changes removeUnderlying() to flushUnderlying() + setUnderlying(NULL, 0).

undesired_operation.description: In the palette RLE path, index is read from the stream and masked to 0-127 via 'index &= 127'. Only palSize entries of palette are initialized. When index >= palSize, palette[index] reads uninitialized stack memory which is written to the pixel buffer.
undesired_operation.impact: Information disclosure - uninitialized stack memory contents are leaked into the framebuffer, potentially exposing sensitive data.

step_by_step_execution.description: index read from stream and masked to 0-127

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: Attacker can send index >= palSize to access uninitialized palette entry

judgments.reasoning: This finding describes an uninitialized palette memory read, which is unrelated to CVE-2019-15691. The actual CVE is about stack use-after-return in ZlibInStream where removeUnderlying() could access freed stack memory when called during exception unwinding. The fix replaces removeUnderlying() with flushUnderlying() + setUnderlying(NULL, 0) and moves the NULL check for the underlying stream.

actual_issue_summary: CVE-2019-15691 is a stack use-after-return vulnerability in ZlibInStream. When ZRLEDecoder (or other decoders) throw an exception, the ZlibInStream destructor calls removeUnderlying(), which could access the underlying stream (a stack-local variable in the caller) that is no longer valid because the stack has already been unwound. The fix replaces removeUnderlying() with flushUnderlying() + setUnderlying(NULL, 0), and moves the NULL check for the underlying pointer from overrun() to decompress(), ensuring the stream pointer is properly nullified and not accessed after it becomes invalid.

cve_desc: The kssl_keytab_is_available function in ssl/kssl.c in OpenSSL before 0.9.8n, when Kerberos is enabled but Kerberos configuration files cannot be opened, does not check a certain return value, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and daemon crash) via SSL cipher negotiation, as demonstrated by a chroot installation of Dovecot or stunnel without Kerberos configuration files inside the chroot.
diff:
--- vulnerable
+++ fixed
@@ -31,6 +31,9 @@
                                      kssl_ctx->service_name ? kssl_ctx->service_name: KRB5SVC,
                                      KRB5_NT_SRV_HST, &princ);
 
+    if (krb5rc)
+	goto exit;
+
     krb5rc = krb5_kt_get_entry(krb5context, krb5keytab, 
                                 princ,
                                 0 /* IGNORE_VNO */,

undesired_operation.description: krb5_kt_get_entry is called with a potentially NULL or invalid 'princ' because the return value of krb5_sname_to_principal is not checked before proceeding. If krb5_sname_to_principal fails, princ may be NULL, leading to a NULL pointer dereference inside krb5_kt_get_entry.
code_snippets:
krb5rc = krb5_kt_get_entry(krb5context, krb5keytab, 
                                princ,
                                0 /* IGNORE_VNO */,
                                0 /* IGNORE_ENCTYPE */,
                                &entry);
undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service via NULL pointer dereference crash. If krb5_sname_to_principal fails (e.g., due to DNS resolution failure or misconfiguration), the NULL princ is passed to krb5_kt_get_entry which will dereference it.

step_by_step_execution.description: krb5_init_context succeeds, setting krb5context to a valid context

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: krb5_init_context succeeded so krb5rc is 0, we do not return early

step_by_step_execution.description: krb5_kt_default or krb5_kt_resolve succeeds, setting krb5keytab
krb5_sname_to_principal is called but fails (e.g., Kerberos config files missing in chroot), returning error and leaving princ as NULL
The return value krb5rc is NOT checked — execution falls through to krb5_kt_get_entry with NULL princ
krb5_kt_get_entry is called with NULL princ, causing NULL pointer dereference and crash

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches CVE-2010-0433: the return value of krb5_sname_to_principal is not checked, allowing a NULL princ to be passed to krb5_kt_get_entry, causing a NULL pointer dereference and daemon crash. The fix adds exactly the missing check.

actual_issue_summary: The return value of krb5_sname_to_principal() was not checked before using princ in the subsequent call to krb5_kt_get_entry(). When Kerberos configuration files are unavailable (e.g., in a chroot), krb5_sname_to_principal fails and princ remains NULL, leading to a NULL pointer dereference and crash. The fix adds a check on krb5rc after krb5_sname_to_principal and jumps to exit on failure.

cve_desc: Multiple double free vulnerabilities in the (1) agent_sign_data function in agent.c, (2) channel_request function in channels.c, (3) ssh_userauth_pubkey function in auth.c, (4) sftp_parse_attr_3 function in sftp.c, and (5) try_publickey_from_file function in keyfiles.c in libssh before 0.5.3 allow remote attackers to cause a denial of service (crash) and possibly execute arbitrary code via unspecified vectors.

undesired_operation.description: When any of the buffer_add or sftp_packet_write calls fail, ssh_buffer_free(buffer) and ssh_string_free(path) are called inside the if-block, then called again unconditionally after the if-block, resulting in a double-free.
undesired_operation.impact: Double-free can corrupt heap metadata, potentially leading to arbitrary code execution or denial of service via crash.

step_by_step_execution.description: buffer_add_u32, buffer_add_ssh_string, buffer_add_attributes, or sftp_packet_write returns < 0

step_by_step_execution.condition: buffer_add_u32(buffer, id) < 0 || buffer_add_ssh_string(buffer, path) < 0 || buffer_add_attributes(buffer, &attr) < 0 || sftp_packet_write(sftp, SSH_FXP_MKDIR, buffer) < 0
step_by_step_execution.reasoning: One of the operations failed, entering the error-handling if-block

step_by_step_execution.description: ssh_buffer_free(buffer) and ssh_string_free(path) are called inside the if-block, freeing both
Execution falls through to ssh_buffer_free(buffer) and ssh_string_free(path) after the if-block, freeing both again (double-free)

judgments.reasoning: The finding exactly matches the CVE-2012-4559 vulnerability in sftp_mkdir: a missing return statement after freeing buffer and path in the error path causes them to be freed again unconditionally, resulting in a double-free. The fix adds 'return -1' to prevent the fall-through.

actual_issue_summary: In sftp_mkdir, when buffer_add or sftp_packet_write fails, the error handling block frees buffer and path but does not return. Execution falls through to free them again unconditionally, causing a double-free. The fix adds 'return -1' in the error block.

cve_desc: The mkdir procedure of GNU Guile temporarily changed the process' umask to zero. During that time window, in a multithreaded application, other threads could end up creating files with insecure permissions. For example, mkdir without the optional mode argument would create directories as 0777. This is fixed in Guile 2.0.13. Prior versions are affected.

undesired_operation.description: The process umask is temporarily set to 0 between the calls umask(0) and umask(mask). During this window, any other thread in the process that creates files or directories will do so with no umask protection, potentially creating world-readable/writable files.
undesired_operation.impact: In a multi-threaded application, other threads creating files between umask(0) and umask(mask) will create files with overly permissive permissions (e.g., world-writable), leading to potential privilege escalation or information disclosure.

initial_state.value: SCM_UNBNDP (unbound, i.e., optional argument not provided)

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: mode is not provided by caller, so SCM_UNBNDP returns true and we enter the if-branch

step_by_step_execution.description: umask(0) is called, setting process umask to 0 and returning the old umask value
Between umask(0) and umask(mask), the process umask is 0. Any other thread creating files during this window will create them with no umask protection.
umask(mask) restores the original umask

judgments.reasoning: This finding correctly identifies the CVE-2016-8605 vulnerability: the temporary zeroing of the process umask via umask(0)/umask(mask) creates a race condition window where other threads can create files with insecure permissions. The fix removes these umask calls entirely, passing 0777 directly to mkdir and letting the kernel apply the umask atomically.

actual_issue_summary: The mkdir procedure temporarily set the process umask to 0 via umask(0) to read the current umask value, then restored it with umask(mask). In multithreaded applications, this race window allowed other threads to create files/directories with no umask protection (e.g., 0777 permissions). The fix removes the umask manipulation entirely, passing 0777 directly to mkdir() and relying on the kernel to apply the umask atomically.

cve_desc: Integer underflow in the unlzw function in unlzw.c in gzip before 1.4 on 64-bit platforms, as used in ncompress and probably others, allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly execute arbitrary code via a crafted archive that uses LZW compression, leading to an array index error.

undesired_operation.description: The while loop that traverses the LZW prefix chain decrements stackp without any lower bound check. With a crafted LZW stream that builds increasingly long prefix chains, stackp can be decremented past the beginning of the de_stack buffer (which is at the end of d_buf, a buffer of DIST_BUFSIZE unsigned shorts = ~16384 bytes), causing out-of-bounds writes below the buffer.
undesired_operation.impact: Arbitrary code execution or denial of service via crash. An attacker can craft a compressed file whose LZW data builds prefix chains longer than the stack buffer, causing writes to memory before the buffer.

judgments.reasoning: The CVE (CVE-2010-0001) is specifically about an integer underflow in the computation `e = insize - (o = (posbits>>3))`. On 64-bit platforms, when `o` (posbits>>3) exceeds `insize`, the subtraction underflows, producing a very large value for `e`, which then causes the subsequent buffer copy loop `for (i = 0; i < e; ++i) inbuf[i] = inbuf[i+o]` to access memory out of bounds. The fix adds a check: `e = o <= insize ? insize - o : 0`. The finding instead describes an unrelated issue with the prefix chain traversal loop decrementing stackp without bounds, which is not what the CVE or the fix addresses.

actual_issue_summary: Integer underflow in `e = insize - (o = (posbits>>3))` in the unlzw function. On 64-bit platforms, when `posbits>>3` exceeds `insize`, the subtraction wraps to a very large positive value, causing the subsequent buffer copy loop to read/write far beyond the `inbuf` buffer bounds. The fix adds a bounds check: `e = o <= insize ? insize - o : 0`.

cve_desc: The binary MP4Box in Gpac 1.0.1 has a null pointer dereference vulnerability in the gf_isom_get_payt_count function in hint_track.c, which allows attackers to cause a denial of service.

undesired_operation.description: hmhd is dereferenced via hmhd->type before the NULL check (!hmhd), causing a null pointer dereference if trak->Media->information->InfoHeader is NULL
undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service via crash (segmentation fault) when InfoHeader is NULL

initial_state.value: NULL (cast from trak->Media->information->InfoHeader which is NULL)

step_by_step_execution.description: hmhd is assigned from trak->Media->information->InfoHeader, which is NULL

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: hmhd is NULL, so dereferencing hmhd->type causes a null pointer dereference before any branch can be taken

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches CVE-2021-40576: a null pointer dereference of hmhd before the NULL check. The fix adds !hmhd to the first condition, which is precisely what the diff shows.

actual_issue_summary: In GetHintFormat(), hmhd (cast from InfoHeader) is dereferenced via hmhd->type on line 4 before checking whether hmhd is NULL on line 7. If InfoHeader is NULL, this causes a null pointer dereference. The fix adds a !hmhd check before the dereference.

cve_desc: Persistent cross-site scripting (XSS) in http/cervlet.c in Tildeslash Monit before 5.25.3 allows a remote unauthenticated attacker to introduce arbitrary JavaScript via manipulation of an unsanitized user field of the Authorization header for HTTP Basic Authentication, which is mishandled during an _viewlog operation.

undesired_operation.description: Log file contents are written into an HTML <textarea> without HTML entity escaping. If the log contains the string '</textarea>', it closes the textarea element, allowing subsequent content from the log to be interpreted as raw HTML/JavaScript by the browser.
undesired_operation.impact: Stored XSS — an attacker who can influence log file content (e.g., through monitored service names, failed login usernames, or other logged input) can execute arbitrary JavaScript in the browser of any administrator viewing the log page, potentially stealing session cookies or performing actions on their behalf.

Monit is configured to log to a file, not syslog
The log file exists and is readable

step_by_step_execution.description: fread reads up to 511 bytes from the log file into buf

out_state.value: Contains raw log content, potentially including </textarea><script>alert(1)</script>

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: Data was read from the log file

in_state.value: Raw log content with possible HTML/JS payloads

out_state.value: Contains unescaped attacker-controlled content rendered as HTML

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the same XSS vulnerability as CVE-2019-11454. The CVE describes persistent XSS via unsanitized content in the _viewlog operation, and the finding pinpoints the exact line where log content is appended without HTML escaping. The fix replaces StringBuffer_append with escapeHTML, confirming the finding.

actual_issue_summary: The do_viewlog function outputs log file contents directly into HTML without escaping. An attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript by getting malicious content into the log file (e.g., via a crafted Authorization header username for HTTP Basic Auth). The fix replaces the raw StringBuffer_append with escapeHTML to properly encode the log content.

cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. Under certain scenarios, Grappler component of TensorFlow can trigger a null pointer dereference. There are 2 places where this can occur, for the same malicious alteration of a `SavedModel` file (fixing the first one would trigger the same dereference in the second place). First, during constant folding, the `GraphDef` might not have the required nodes for the binary operation. If a node is missing, the correposning `mul_*child` would be null, and the dereference in the subsequent line would be incorrect. We have a similar issue during `IsIdentityConsumingSwitch`. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

undesired_operation.description: node_map_->GetNode() may return nullptr for mul_left_child or mul_right_child, which are immediately dereferenced without null checks via IsReallyConstant(*mul_left_child) and IsReallyConstant(*mul_right_child)
undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service via crash (null pointer dereference) if a graph node referenced by the Mul node's inputs does not exist in node_map_

step_by_step_execution.description: node_map_->GetNode(node->input(0)) is called, which may return nullptr if the node referenced by input(0) doesn't exist in the graph
node_map_->GetNode(node->input(1)) is called, which may return nullptr if the node referenced by input(1) doesn't exist in the graph
IsReallyConstant(*mul_left_child) dereferences mul_left_child which is nullptr, causing a crash

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches the CVE-2022-23589 vulnerability. The diff adds a null check for mul_left_child and mul_right_child immediately after GetNode() calls, which is precisely what the finding identifies as missing.

actual_issue_summary: In ConstantFolding::MulConvPushDown, node_map_->GetNode() can return nullptr for mul_left_child or mul_right_child when the GraphDef has missing nodes (e.g., from a malicious SavedModel). These pointers are dereferenced without null checks, causing a null pointer dereference crash. The fix adds a null check returning false if either pointer is null.

cve_desc: A Segmentation fault caused by a null pointer dereference vulnerability exists in Gpac through 1.0.1 via the gf_avc_parse_nalu function in av_parsers.c when using mp4box, which causes a denial of service.

undesired_operation.description: Null pointer dereference on avc->s_info.sps when assert() is compiled out in release builds. The code dereferences avc->s_info.sps->poc_type after only an assert guard, which is removed when NDEBUG is defined.
undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service via crash (null pointer dereference) when processing a crafted AVC/H.264 bitstream where no SPS has been parsed before a slice NAL unit is encountered.

Execution enters the slice case, slice=1, avc_parse_slice is called and returns >= 0

judgments.reasoning: The finding exactly matches CVE-2021-40565: a null pointer dereference of avc->s_info.sps in gf_avc_parse_nalu. The fix replaced the assert with a proper null check that returns -1, which is precisely what the diff shows.

actual_issue_summary: In gf_avc_parse_nalu, avc->s_info.sps could be NULL when reached in the slice parsing case. The code only had an assert() guard (no-op in release builds), so dereferencing avc->s_info.sps->poc_type caused a null pointer dereference and crash. The fix replaced the assert with a proper null check returning -1.

cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. TensorFlow's type inference can cause a heap out of bounds read as the bounds checking is done in a `DCHECK` (which is a no-op during production). An attacker can control the `input_idx` variable such that `ix` would be larger than the number of values in `node_t.args`. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. This is the only affected version.

undesired_operation.description: In release builds, DCHECK is a no-op. The index 'ix' (derived from edge->src_output()) is used to access node_t.args(ix) on a protobuf repeated field without any runtime bounds check. If ix >= node_t.args_size(), this performs an out-of-bounds read on the protobuf's internal array.
undesired_operation.impact: Out-of-bounds read on protobuf internal array. An attacker who can construct a graph with mismatched src_output indices and type annotations can read memory beyond the repeated field's bounds, potentially causing a crash (denial of service) or information disclosure.

initial_state.value: vector of size input_types.size(), initialized to 0
vector of size input_types.size(), initialized to nullptr

step_by_step_execution.description: Edges are iterated; for non-control edges, input_idx[i] is set to edge->src_output()

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: All input nodes are present, so the function does not bail early
The input node has type information set
The type is set to a real type, not TFT_UNSET

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: DCHECK is a no-op in production/release builds, so this check is never actually evaluated

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches CVE-2022-23592: the DCHECK bounds check on ix is a no-op in production, allowing an attacker-controlled input_idx to cause an out-of-bounds read via node_t.args(ix). The CWE, impact, and code location all match the CVE.

actual_issue_summary: In TensorFlow's type inference (Node::RunForwardTypeInference), the bounds check on the 'ix' index into node_t.args() was performed only via DCHECK, which is disabled in production builds. An attacker could craft a graph where input_idx values exceed the number of type args, causing a heap out-of-bounds read. The fix replaces the DCHECK with a proper runtime bounds check that logs a warning and returns early.

cve_desc: PJSIP is a free and open source multimedia communication library written in the C language. Versions 2.12 and prior contain a denial-of-service vulnerability that affects PJSIP users that consume PJSIP's XML parsing in their apps. Users are advised to update. There are no known workarounds.

undesired_operation.description: In the CDATA parsing section, when the scanner cursor is positioned at a ']' character but the subsequent characters do not form ']]>', the while loop calls pj_scan_get_until_ch(scanner, ']', &dummy) which returns immediately without advancing curptr (since curptr is already at ']'). This creates an infinite loop as the loop condition never changes.
undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service — an attacker can craft XML input with a CDATA section containing a ']' not followed by ']>' to cause the parser to hang indefinitely, consuming CPU resources.

initial_state.value: points to start of XML input containing a CDATA section with ']' not followed by ']>'

step_by_step_execution.description: Parser reaches CDATA section and advances past '<![CDATA[' prefix
pj_scan_get_until_ch(scanner, ']', &node->content) scans until first ']'

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: The ']' is not followed by ']>' (e.g. it's followed by something else like 'x'), so the strcmp does not match ']]>'

step_by_step_execution.description: pj_scan_get_until_ch(scanner, ']', &dummy) is called but curptr already points to ']', so it returns immediately without advancing

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: curptr hasn't moved, so the same comparison fails again, creating an infinite loop

judgments.reasoning: The finding exactly matches the CVE fix. The diff adds pj_scan_advance_n(scanner, 1, PJ_FALSE) before pj_scan_get_until_ch in the CDATA while loop to prevent the infinite loop when curptr is already at ']' but ']]>' doesn't match. The finding correctly identifies this as an infinite loop (CWE-835) causing denial of service, which aligns with the CVE description of a DoS in XML parsing.

actual_issue_summary: Infinite loop in CDATA XML parsing: when scanning CDATA content, if a ']' character is encountered but not followed by ']>', the while loop repeatedly calls pj_scan_get_until_ch(scanner, ']', &dummy) which doesn't advance past the current ']', causing an infinite loop. The fix adds pj_scan_advance_n(scanner, 1, PJ_FALSE) to skip past the non-matching ']' before scanning for the next one.

cve_desc: There is an assertion failure in SingleComponentLSScan::ParseMCU in singlecomponentlsscan.cpp in libjpeg before 1.64 via an empty JPEG-LS scan.

undesired_operation.description: When m_ucCount >= 4, the loops iterate cx from 0 to m_ucCount-1, writing past the bounds of multiple stack-allocated arrays of size 4 (line[4], lp[4], a[4], b[4], c[4], d[4], d1[4], d2[4], d3[4]). The assert(m_ucCount < 4) is compiled out in release builds and provides no protection.
undesired_operation.impact: Stack buffer overflow leading to arbitrary code execution. An attacker can craft a JPEG-LS image with a scan header specifying 4 or more components, causing out-of-bounds writes on multiple stack arrays.

step_by_step_execution.description: m_ucCount is read from the JPEG-LS scan header

judgments.reasoning: This finding describes a hypothetical stack buffer overflow in a multi-component scan function with arrays of size 4, but the actual vulnerable function shown is SingleComponentLSScan::ParseMCU which has no such arrays. The CVE (CVE-2022-32978) is about an assertion failure (`assert(lines > 0)`) triggered when an empty JPEG-LS scan causes `lines` to be 0. The fix replaces the assert with a graceful `if (lines == 0) return false;`. The finding describes an entirely different issue in a different function.

actual_issue_summary: In SingleComponentLSScan::ParseMCU, when an empty JPEG-LS scan results in `lines` being 0, the code hits `assert(lines > 0)` which causes an assertion failure (crash/denial of service) in debug builds, or proceeds into a do-while loop with lines=0 causing undefined behavior in release builds. The fix replaces the assert with an early return when lines==0.

cve_desc: The kernel in Amazon Web Services FreeRTOS before 10.4.3 has an integer overflow in queue.c for queue creation.

undesired_operation.impact: Heap buffer overflow — the allocated buffer is much smaller than expected due to addition overflow. Queue operations write past the allocated boundary, potentially enabling arbitrary code execution.

cve_desc: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions the implementation of `tf.raw_ops.ResourceScatterDiv` is vulnerable to a division by 0 error. The [implementation](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/8d72537c6abf5a44103b57b9c2e22c14f5f49698/tensorflow/core/kernels/resource_variable_ops.cc#L865) uses a common class for all binary operations but fails to treat the division by 0 case separately. We have patched the issue in GitHub commit 4aacb30888638da75023e6601149415b39763d76. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.6.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.5.1, TensorFlow 2.4.3, and TensorFlow 2.3.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

undesired_operation.description: params->dim_size(0) is called without verifying that params has at least 1 dimension, potentially causing out-of-bounds access on shape dimensions array for scalar tensors.
undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service via crash or potential information disclosure from out-of-bounds read on the shape's internal dimension array when a scalar variable resource is provided as params.

initial_state.value: pointer to tensor from resource variable, could be scalar (0-dim)

step_by_step_execution.description: params is obtained from resource variable lookup
params->dim_size(0) is called without checking params->dims() >= 1

out_state.value: out-of-bounds access on shape dimensions array

judgments.reasoning: The CVE is specifically about division by zero (CWE-369) in ResourceScatterDiv when the updates tensor contains zero values. The fix adds a check to validate that updates do not contain 0 when the operation is DIV. This finding describes a different issue (potential out-of-bounds read on scalar params) which is unrelated to the division-by-zero vulnerability that is the subject of CVE-2021-37642.

actual_issue_summary: The ResourceScatterDiv operation fails to check whether the updates tensor contains zero values before performing division, leading to a floating-point exception (division by zero). The fix adds validation that rejects updates containing 0 when the scatter operation is DIV on CPU devices.

cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. An attacker can craft a TFLite model that would cause an integer overflow in `TfLiteIntArrayCreate`. The `TfLiteIntArrayGetSizeInBytes` returns an `int` instead of a `size_t. An attacker can control model inputs such that `computed_size` overflows the size of `int` datatype. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

undesired_operation.description: Integer overflow in the computation `sizeof(dummy) + sizeof(dummy.data[0]) * size`. When `size` is a large attacker-controlled int, the intermediate size_t result of the multiplication/addition wraps when truncated back to the `int` return type (`computed_size`), producing a small positive value. This undersized value is returned to the caller and typically used for memory allocation, leading to a heap buffer overflow when the caller populates the array.
undesired_operation.impact: Heap buffer overflow — the function returns a small allocation size for a logically large array, leading to out-of-bounds writes when the caller fills the array. This can lead to arbitrary code execution.

state.value: large attacker-controlled int value causing overflow when computing allocation size
small positive int after truncation from size_t

initial_state.value: attacker-controlled int, can be very large

step_by_step_execution.description: TfLiteIntArrayGetSizeInBytes(size) computes sizeof(dummy) + sizeof(dummy.data[0]) * size. With a large size, the result overflows when stored in int return type.

out_state.value: small positive int due to overflow/truncation

step_by_step_execution.description: The overflowed return value is assigned to int alloc_size in TfLiteIntArrayCreate

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: alloc_size is a small positive value due to overflow, so the check passes and execution continues to malloc

step_by_step_execution.description: malloc(alloc_size) allocates a small buffer, but the caller will write size entries into it, causing heap buffer overflow

judgments.reasoning: This finding directly describes the CVE-2022-23558 vulnerability: TfLiteIntArrayGetSizeInBytes returns an int, causing integer overflow when size is large. The overflowed value is stored in an int alloc_size, leading to an undersized allocation and subsequent heap buffer overflow. The fix changes alloc_size to size_t to preserve the full computed size.

actual_issue_summary: TfLiteIntArrayGetSizeInBytes returns an int, and the result is stored in an int variable `alloc_size` in TfLiteIntArrayCreate. When the input `size` is large, the computed allocation size overflows the int type, resulting in a small positive value that passes the <= 0 check. This leads to an undersized malloc allocation. The fix changes `alloc_size` from `int` to `size_t`.

cve_desc: The binary MP4Box in Gpac 1.0.1 has a double-free vulnerability in the gf_text_get_utf8_line function in load_text.c, which allows attackers to cause a denial of service, even code execution and escalation of privileges.

undesired_operation.description: strcpy(szLine, szLineConv) copies the szLineConv buffer back into szLine without checking that lineSize >= strlen(szLineConv)+1. In the UTF-16 conversion path (unicode_type 2 or 3), UTF-16 to UTF-8 expansion can produce a string longer than lineSize, overflowing szLine.
undesired_operation.impact: Heap or stack buffer overflow (depending on where szLine is allocated) if lineSize < length of converted string in szLineConv. Could lead to arbitrary code execution.

judgments.reasoning: The actual vulnerability fixed by this commit is a stack buffer overflow of szLineConv[1024] itself. The fix increases szLineConv from 1024 to 2048 bytes and passes 2048 to gf_utf8_wcstombs. The finding instead identifies a potential overflow of szLine via strcpy, which is a different buffer and not what the fix addresses. The CVE is about the undersized szLineConv buffer being overflowed during UTF-8 conversion, not about szLine being overflowed by strcpy.

actual_issue_summary: Stack buffer overflow in szLineConv[1024]: the buffer was too small for UTF-16 to UTF-8 conversion output. The fix increases szLineConv to 2048 bytes and passes the correct size to gf_utf8_wcstombs. The commit also fixes an unrelated buffer overflow in gf_text_process_sub.

cve_desc: MP4Box is a component of GPAC-2.0.0, which is a widely-used third-party package on RPM Fusion. When MP4Box tries to parse a MP4 file, it calls the function `diST_box_read()` to read from video. In this function, it allocates a buffer `str` with fixed length. However, content read from `bs` is controllable by user, so is the length, which causes a buffer overflow.

undesired_operation.description: The while loop reads bytes from the bitstream into the stack buffer str[1024] without checking that index i stays within bounds. If the bitstream contains more than 1023 non-null bytes before a null terminator, the write str[i] overflows the buffer.
undesired_operation.impact: Stack buffer overflow leading to potential arbitrary code execution or crash. An attacker who controls the input file can write arbitrary data past the 1024-byte stack buffer.

step_by_step_execution.description: i is initialized to 0, str[0] is set to 0
Loop reads bytes from bs into str[i] one at a time, incrementing i each iteration. Attacker controls content of bs.

out_state.value: increments with each non-null byte read

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: There is no such bounds check in the code. The loop only terminates when a null byte is read. If the attacker provides >= 1024 non-null bytes, i grows past 1023 and writes continue past the buffer.

step_by_step_execution.description: str[i] = gf_bs_read_u8(bs) writes beyond the 1024-byte buffer boundary

out_state.value: attacker-controlled byte written out of bounds

judgments.reasoning: The finding directly describes the CVE-2022-1441 vulnerability: the fixed-size 1024-byte stack buffer str can be overflowed because the while loop has no bounds check on i, and the attacker controls the bitstream content. This matches the CVE description exactly.

actual_issue_summary: In diST_box_read(), a fixed-size 1024-byte stack buffer is used to read user-controlled data from the bitstream in an unbounded loop, causing a stack buffer overflow when input exceeds 1024 bytes. The fix replaces the fixed buffer with a dynamically allocated buffer sized according to the box size.

cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. The implementation of `FractionalMaxPool` can be made to crash a TensorFlow process via a division by 0. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

undesired_operation.description: The outer loop iterates over input_size[0] (batch dimension) but computes out_offset into the output tensor which has output_size[0] batches. When pooling_ratio_[0] > 1.0, output_size[0] < input_size[0], causing out_mat.col(out_offset) to access beyond the allocated output tensor buffer (heap buffer overflow).
code_snippets: const int64_t out_offset = (b * output_size[1] + hs) * output_size[2] + ws;
undesired_operation.impact: Heap buffer overflow write — an attacker who can control pooling_ratio_[0] to be > 1.0 causes writes past the end of the output tensor buffer, potentially achieving arbitrary code execution or denial of service.

judgments.reasoning: This finding identifies a consequence of the same missing validation (pooling_ratio > input_size) that the CVE fix addresses. While the CVE is described as division by zero, the root cause is the same: no check that input_size[i] >= pooling_ratio_[i], and the fix is the same added OP_REQUIRES check.

undesired_operation.description: DCHECK_GT(output_size[i], 0) is a debug-only assertion. In release builds, when pooling_ratio_[i] > input_size[i], output_size[i] becomes 0. The output tensor is allocated with a zero-sized dimension (0 total elements). If GeneratePoolingSequence returns a sequence with more than 1 element for output_size=0, the inner loops execute and write to out_mat.col() on a zero-element buffer, causing heap buffer overflow.
undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service or heap buffer overflow — attacker sets pooling_ratio for a dimension larger than the input dimension size, causing output_size to be 0 and potential OOB writes in release builds.
